revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:乳岡古墳]]





{{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]]
|name = Chino-oka Kofun
|image = File:Chinooka Kofun.JPG
| part_of = {{ill|Mozu Tombs|qid=Q4250584|1=Mozu Tombs}}
|location = {{ill|Sakai|qid=Q193428|1=Sakai}}
|coordinates = {{Coord|34.555861|N|135.465444|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}
|map = {{location map|Japan Osaka|map_dot_label=Chino-oka Kofun|coordinates=34|coordinates=33|coordinates=21.1|lon_deg=135|lon_min=27|lon_sec=55.6|omitted=place-of-worship|marksize=10|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Osaka Prefecture|qq=}}
{{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|33|21.1|N|135|27|55.6|E|qq=}}|qq=}}
|type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}}
|area = Kofun length: 155 m
|built = Late 4th century
|burial = {{ill|Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|ja|長持形石棺|1=Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagamochi-type sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}}
|Interred_individual = Unknown
|burial goods = Jade comma-shaped stone, wheel-shaped stone burial goods
|designation1 = National Historic Site (Historic Site1 1974)
|architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}}
}}
[[Chin'ooka Kofun|'''Chin'ooka Kofun''']] (乳岡古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} located in {{ill|Sakai-ku, Sakai|ja|堺市堺区|1=Sakai-ku, Sakai|2=ja|3=堺区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakai-ku, Sakai|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1042008}}, Osaka Prefecture. It is part of the {{ill|Mozu Tombs|en|Mozu Tombs|qid=Q4250584|lt=Mozu Tombs|1=Mozu Tombs}}, which has been Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site.
== Overview ==
The tumulus is located in the southwestern coastal area of the Mozu Kofun Cluster, on land approximately 6 meters above sea level, with the front part of the tumulus oriented southwest. Much of the front part was destroyed in the early Shōwa period, but the original length is estimated to have been 155 meters. The rear part remains partially intact, constructed in three tiers with a diameter of 94 meters and a height of 14 meters. While the surrounding moat is now filled in, archaeological investigations have confirmed the presence of a 30-meter-wide moat on the western side of the rear mound.
In 1703, the rear mound became the site of the {{ill|Iwatsuta Shrine|lt=Iwatsuta Shrine|lt=Ishizuta Shrine|ja|石津太神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishizuta Shrine|qq=}}’s temple, {{ill|Nenbutsu-ji (Sakai)|ja|念仏寺 (堺市)|lt=Nenbutsu-ji|Nenbutsu-ji=Nenbutsu-ji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nenbutsu-ji (Sakai)|qq=}}, nicknamed "The Temple on the Hill." After archaeological surveys, the temple was relocated. The flat area on the tumulus summit was modified during temple construction, which likely altered the original Kofun.
Records from the Edo period describe the heights of the southern (front) and northern (rear) parts of the tumulus as 7.2 meters and 12.6 meters, respectively. The tumulus was traditionally associated with {{ill|Nomi no Sukune|en|Nomi no Sukune|qid=Q965742|lt=Nomi no Sukune|1=Nomi no Sukune}} or the Chichi Asomi clan.
== Internal burial and built ==
An excavation conducted in 1972 confirmed that the mound was covered in fukiishi (paving stones) and adorned with haniwa. A {{ill|Nomi no Sukune|ja|長持形石棺|1=Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nomi no Sukune|13=User:Immanuelle/Chest-shaped Sarcophagus|qq=}} was found in the clay-covered center of the rear mound. The sarcophagus, made from soft stone, is composed of six parts: lid stone, base stone, and four side stones. Its area, with a lid measuring 86 cm long and 75.3 cm wide, are unusually small compared to other stone sarcophagi in tumuli of similar size, suggesting that it may not be the central burial or could represent an early type of the nagamochi-style sarcophagus.
burial goods such as three jade comma-shaped stones and 18 wheel-shaped stones were discovered near the sarcophagus, alongside small amounts of finned haniwa from the surrounding moat. Based on the burial goods, structure, and location, the tumulus is dated to the late 4th century, making it one of the earliest in the Mozu Kofun Cluster.
== area ==
The area of the tumulus are as follows:
* Total length: 155 meters
* Rear mound diameter: 94 meters
* Rear mound height: 14 meters
== designation1 ==
=== National Historic Site ===
Chino-oka Kofun was Historic Site1 as part of the National Historic Site of the Mozu Kofun Cluster in 1974. Subsequent additions and expansions have included other kofun within the cluster.
== References ==
{{Reflist|qq=}}
== Further Reading ==
* {{Cite book|title=日本古墳大辞典 (Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun)|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|year=1989|qq=}}
* {{Cite book|title=図説日本の史跡 第2巻 原始2 (Illustrated Japanese Historic Sites, Vol. 2, Prehistoric 2)|publisher=Dōbōsha|year=1991|qq=}}
* {{Cite book|title=百舌鳥古墳群 -堺の文化財- 第7版 (Mozu Kofun Cluster - Cultural Assets of Sakai, 7th Edition)|publisher=Department of Historic Site, Sakai City|year=2014|qq=}}
== External Links ==
* [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/database/chinooka.html Chino-oka Kofun - Sakai City Cultural Tourism Department]
* [https://www.sakai-tcb.or.jp/spot/detail/105 Chino-oka Kofun - Sakai Tourism and Convention Bureau]
* [https://www.lib-sakai.jp/kyoudo/sakai_taikan/sakaiTaikan_52.htm image of the rear mound and Nenbutsu-ji - Sakai City Library]
